vulnerability.title: Disable acquiring, saving, and processing core dumps
vulnerability.description: The systemd-coredump.socket unit is a socket activation of the systemd-coredump@.service which processes core dumps. By masking the unit, core dump processing is disabled.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then SOCKET_NAME="systemd-coredump.socket" SYSTEMCTL_EXEC='/usr/bin/systemctl' if "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" -q list-unit-files --type socket | grep -q "$SOCKET_NAME"; then if [[ $("$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" is-system-running) != "offline" ]]; then "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" stop "$SOCKET_NAME" fi "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" mask "$SOCKET_NAME" fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Masking the systemd-coredump.socket unit is a standard hardening step that disables core dump processing without affecting authentication, networking, or filesystem integrity. It does not risk service lockout or system breakage.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to display plan first thru review_plan. Provide plan description: mask systemd-coredump.socket via systemctl mask. But only use run_cmd tool. Also must read_file to inspect config? No config file. So we just run systemctl mask command. But must check conditions: check if socket exists and system running.

So plan: 1. Check if kernel-core package installed. 2. Check if systemd-coredump.socket unit file present via systemctl list-unit-files. 3. If present and system not offline, stop it. Then mask it. Use individual commands. We need to read in python? But we can use run_cmd to get output.

We will call review_plan with plan description.

Proceed.

review_feedback: The remediation plan will not effectively address the vulnerability if executed without root privileges. Systemd unit masking requires root authentication; the commands as written will fail when run by an unprivileged user. The plan also assumes the presence of the systemctl binary in the default PATH and does not handle potential error conditions.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation plan will not effectively address the vulnerability if executed without root privileges. Systemd unit masking requires root authentication; the commands as written will fail when run by an unprivileged user. The plan also assumes the presence of the systemctl binary in the default PATH and does not handle potential error conditions.
concerns: Missing root privileges; systemctl mask requires sudo/root
suggested_improvements: Use 'sudo systemctl' or ensure the script runs as root. Explicitly specify full path to systemctl (/usr/bin/systemctl). Handle non-zero exit statuses and print informative errors. Verify that systemd-coredump.socket is actually in use before stopping/masking to avoid interrupting legitimate core dump usage. Consider adding a backup or audit log entry. Use 'systemctl list-unit-files --type socket' and 'systemctl status systemd-coredump.socket' to confirm state before actions.
